name: Smoked Salmon Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small glass measure, sprinkle gelatin over cold water. Let stand 5 minutes to soften.
2. Microwave at High for 1 minute or until dissolved.
3. Using a knife, pierce the top of the spinach package in several places and place on a plate. Microwave at High for 5 minutes.
4. Cool. Squeeze out as much moisture as possible.
5. In a processor, blend cream cheese with sour cream until smooth.
6. Add smoked salmon and dill; process using on-off turns until coarsely chopped.
7. Add lemon juice, paprika, pepper, and dissolved gelatin. Process for a few seconds until mixed.
8. Remove all but 1 cup of the mixture from the processor and set aside.
9. To the remaining 1 cup mixture, add spinach and process until smooth.
10. Divide the reserved smoked salmon mixture in 2.
11. Evenly spread one half of the smoked salmon mixture into an oiled 6-cup ring mold or small loaf pan, lined with plastic wrap.
12. Evenly spread the spinach mixture for the middle layer.
13. Top with the remaining smoked salmon mixture.
14. Cover and refrigerate until set, about 3 hours to overnight.
15. To unmold, loosen edges with a knife and dip quickly in hot water. Unmold onto a serving platter.
16. Garnish with lemon slices and dill sprigs. Serve with crackers and thinly sliced pumpernickel bread.
